no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly specifies exact constant names and values (MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE = 8, KEY_BLOCK_ENTRY_TYPE_INLINE_MIN = 8), exact method names (put_inline, slice_from_subslice, entry_val_size), exact enum variant names (EntryValue::Inline), exact return types (LookupValue::Slice), and even implementation details (type = 8 + value_length, use extend_from_slice or copy_from_slice). solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex to assert exact constant names (MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE, KEY_BLOCK_ENTRY_TYPE_INLINE_MIN), exact method names (put_inline, slice_from_subslice, entry_val_size), exact enum variant name (Inline in EntryValue), and specific string patterns (extend_from_slice/copy_from_slice, INLINE_MIN). An alternative correct implementation using different naming conventions (e.g., INLINE_VALUE_MAX_SIZE, write_inline, create_subslice) would fail these tests despite being functionally correct.

They check for string patterns like 'slice_from_subslice' and 'extend_from_slice' in source text. The pass-to-pass tests do compile/test t

test_not_tautological.reason: All fail-to-pass tests are text/regex checks on source files. A developer could satisfy them by adding stub functions and constants that match the regex patterns without implementing any actual inline value behavior. For example, adding 'pub const MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE: usize = 8;' to constants.rs, an empty 'Inline' variant to EntryValue, and stub functions with the right names containing the searched keywords would pass all f2p tests without functional implementation — as long as cargo check/te

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires updating the README (documented in point 7 of Expected Behavior), but there are no tests verifying the README update — the config_edit test section is empty. Also, no behavioral test verifies that inline values are actually written and read correctly end-to-end.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.
